prompt: Search X for 'someone should build' / 'why is there no' / 'I'd pay for' posts in 2026 related to AI security, agent security, compliance, trust attestation, or passing security reviews. Quote verbatim with handles. Cite.
content:
**No exact matches for the specific phrases ("someone should build", "why is there no", or "I'd pay for") paired directly with the listed topics were found via keyword search on X in 2026.**[[1]](https://x.com/shahabjaji/status/2060466332596896109)

Semantic search surfaced several closely related posts from 2026 discussing AI/agent security tools, compliance automation, audits, and related gaps or builds (including one that echoes "why hasn't someone built" language). Here are the most relevant verbatim quotes with handles:

- **@openrunt** (Jun 5, 2026): "I'm curious as to why someone hasn't specifically made public or made available an installable or deployable autonomous agent network that monitors for threats and routes to the appropriate authority 'in the event' a situation is detected. with a liaison or counterpart on the other receiving end. japan has 1pb/s internet speed before this is a thing. GTA 6 will be out before this is a thing. There are fragments though. Enterprise tools like Dropzone AI (Agentic SOC), NetMoniAI (open source distributed agents for real time network monitoring), SentinelOne, Vectra, and Palo Alto's systems already run autonomous agents that detect threats, investigate, and respond inside corporate environments. Governments and AI labs have internal monitoring for AI-specific risks (METR's red-teaming of frontier labs' own agents, CAIS-style risk frameworks, etc.) But nobody has put all of the pieces of the puzzle together in one unified trusted brand."[[2]](https://x.com/openrunt/status/2062722857457664100)

- **@pashov** (Mar 18, 2026): "🤯Protocols would pay >$1000 per run for this. Someone just Open Sourced it for free. MIT Licensed. This AI security tool is proven to get up to 90% coverage post human audits. I haven't seen another one that digs deeper into a codebase. Massive boost for the space🫡"[[3]](https://x.com/pashov/status/2034396314427883735)

- **@polsia** (Jun 2, 2026): "Built Trustlayer today. An AI agent that keeps companies continuously SOC 2 compliant — monitoring controls 24/7, auto-generating evidence, flagging failures before audits. The compliance grind, fully automated."[[4]](https://x.com/polsia/status/2061922817709654099)

- **@chiefofautism** (Mar 5, 2026): "someone built an AI that AUDITS smart contracts AUTONOMOUSLY its called Solidity Auditor multiple sub-agents working together, one scans for vulnerabilities, one generates exploits, one writes the report you point it at any solidity codebase and it finds bugs that cost protocols millions fully open source, runs on your own AI model, costs $0"[[5]](https://x.com/chiefofautism/status/2029476239778464156)

- **@pashov** (Mar 19, 2026): "🧠AI security tools are booming. Still, many are slow, painful to set up or expensive to run. solidity-auditor v2 drops tomorrow. 1 command install. 8 parallel specialist agents. <10min on 5000 nSLOC. Runs locally on a cheap API plan. Free (even though it probably shouldn't be)"[[6]](https://x.com/pashov/status/2034648081216553217)

These reflect demand, gaps, or builds in AI security/auditing and compliance automation. Exact phrase matches on the queried topics appear rare or nonexistent in the 2026 data retrieved.
